stem: In the xy-plane, a parabola has vertex (6, −10) and intersects the x-axis at two points. If the equation of the parabola is written in the form y = ax2 + bx + c, where a, b, and c are constants, which of the following could be the value of a + b + c?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The value of a + b + c equals y at x = 1, since y(1) = a + b + c. Writing the parabola in vertex form y = a(x − 6)2 − 10, we get y(1) = a(25) − 10 = 25a − 10. For the parabola to intersect the x-axis at two distinct points and have vertex below the x-axis, the parabola must open upward, so a > 0. Therefore 25a − 10 > −10. Of the choices, only −8 satisfies this inequality.

Choices A, B, and D are all at most −10 and so cannot equal 25a − 10 for any positive a.
